headline: Donald Trump welcomed in Beijing with pomp and a stern warning
description: For the US president's visit, Xi Jinping put on a display of grandeur, while remaining very firm on the issue of Taiwan, a matter that determines the entire relationship with Washington.

headline: Russian barrage pounds Kyiv, killing more than a dozen
Israel to sue New York Times over report on sexual abuse of Palestinian inmates
A history of failed negotiations between Israel and Lebanon
description: In 1983, a peace agreement was signed between Israel and Lebanon but the Lebanese government annulled it just a few months later under pressure from Syria. New bilateral negotiations took place between 1991 and 1993, following the Madrid Peace Conference, but yielded no results.

headline: School uniforms have 'limited effects on student behavior and learning,' according to initial reports in French experiment
description: The experiment with a 'standard uniform,' launched in 2023 by then education minister Gabriel Attal, has involved about 100 elementary, middle and high schools. The first study on the measure highlights significant differences in perception between adults and students, and found 'modest and mixed' impacts on the school environment.

headline: 2026 World Cup: France reveal their 26-player squad
description: Coach Didier Deschamps on Thursday announced his squad for this summer's tournament in the United States, Canada and Mexico. Kylian Mbappé will likely captain the team despite a thigh injury sustained last month.

headline: With CMA Média, shipping magnate Rodolphe Saadé expands his empire
description: 'The shipping giant at the heart of French power' (4/4). Since 2022, CMA Média, the subsidiary of shipping giant CMA CGM, has multiplied its media acquisitions from La Provence to La Tribune Dimanche, as well as BFM-TV and RMC, and is looking to strengthen its sports division. Journalists have expressed their opposition to consolidation plans and editorial interference.

headline: 'For the first time in its history, humankind has created a being that mimics it in what constitutes its very essence: the ability to think, to create, to decide.'

'Zinédine Zidane maintains a political and social awareness that is deeply connected to his own story'
description: The sociologist Stéphane Beaud explored in an essay the football player's career, framing it within that of the descendants of Algerian immigrants. In an interview, he explains that the athlete tried to make up for the injustices suffered by his father, who remained at the bottom of the social ladder throughout his life.

headline: How one family and their shipping company dominate Marseille
description: 'The shipping giant at the heart of French power' (3/4). Based in France's second-largest city, the company led by Rodolphe Saadé plays a major role in the city but has become less visible since the controversy over excess profits.

headline: French far-right mayors' first steps target Europe, culture and unions
description: Le Monde examined the first decisions made by newly-elected far-right mayors. While they have not yet kept their tax promises, almost all of them have increased their own allowances, hired police officers and targeted local figures who are politically hostile to them.
